title: Mushroom Stew with Cannellini Bean Mash
summary: If you’re looking for wintery comfort food that also improves your gut health, we’ve got you. This mushroom stew with cannellini bean mash feels like a warm hug and is packed with fibre and plant protein.

method.description: Finely slice the shallot and garlic. Slice the mushrooms.

For the bean mash, heat a glug of oil in a medium pan over a medium heat. Add ½ the garlic and ½ the rosemary and thyme sprigs and cook for a minute or two until the garlic starts to turn translucent.
Add the beans with ½ their bean juice. Season with a pinch of salt and simmer for 10 mins until the beans are soft enough to mash with a wooden spoon.

For the mushrooms, heat a glug of olive oil in a separate pan over a medium heat and add the shallot, sage leaf and remaining garlic, rosemary and thyme. Gently fry until the shallots are translucent and slightly caramelised. Add the sliced mushrooms and cook for a further 5 mins.
Add a splash of vegetable stock to the mushrooms then season to taste and tear in the cavolo nero.
Divide the bean mash between plates and top with the mushrooms.

chefs.name: Healthy by ZOE
chefs.summary: ZOE (@ZOE) is a science and nutrition company leading a movement to transform the health of millions through smarter food choices. ZOE means life in Greek — and ZOE passionately believes everyone can feel better and live longer, healthier lives by rethinking what they eat. ZOE conducts the world’s largest nutrition science study to better understand the relationship between food and health. Based on this research, their personalised nutrition program delivers tailored advice based on metabolic and gut health, turning complex science into clear step-by-step actions to improve your health with every meal. Become a member at zoe.com. All of their recipes are developed by nutritionists to help you embrace food that tastes amazing and makes you feel even better.
